data_inventory.name: DUVET galaxy survey
resolution_log: No specific astro-archive matched. Falling back to generic manual resolution.

parameters.name: Redshift threshold
parameters.paper_quote: selected to be at a redshift 𝑧≥0.1

parameters.name: Slit width
parameters.paper_quote: using Low Resolution Imaging Spectrometer (LRIS; Oke et al. 1995) on the Keck telescope with the 0.7′′ slit.

parameters.name: Blue CCD grism
parameters.paper_quote: employed the B1200/3400 grism in the blue CCD

parameters.name: Red CCD grating
parameters.paper_quote: used the R1200/3400 grating with a central wavelength of 7003 Å in the red CCD

parameters.name: Mean blue exposure time (s)
parameters.paper_quote: The mean galaxy exposure times were 6177 s for the blue side

parameters.name: Mean red exposure time (s)
parameters.paper_quote: and 5445 s for the red side.

method_pipeline.name: Spectroscopic Data Reduction

parameters.name: Binning strategy
parameters.paper_quote: The spectra were then spatially binned to match the seeing on the night of observations.

gap_flags: Specific IRAF routines used are not detailed

parameters.name: Number of Gaussians per set
parameters.paper_quote: modelled the H𝛼and [Nii] 𝜆𝜆6548, 6583 emission lines by two sets of three Gaussians to the spectral profiles

parameters.name: Monte Carlo realisations
parameters.paper_quote: For each spectrum, we ran 1000 Monte Carlo realisations

method_pipeline.name: Absorption and Emission Line Modeling (MgII)
outputs: MgII absorption and emission line parameters

method_pipeline.name: Outflow Velocity Calculation
inputs: MgII absorption and emission line parameters

parameters.name: v_out definition
parameters.paper_quote: we define 𝑣out = 𝑣−2𝜎, where 𝑣is the central velocity of the component and 𝜎is the velocity dispersion.

compute_estimate.rationale: The methodology involves standard IRAF data reduction for Keck/LRIS spectra and HST imaging of three galaxies, followed by 1D Gaussian spectral line fitting with 1000 Monte Carlo realizations across approximately 16 spatial bins. This requires minimal CPU time, no GPU acceleration, and only a few gigabytes of storage for the raw and processed observational data.
